_comment: Configuration for the jscpd copy-paste detector used by the 'Code duplication gate'
step in .github/workflows/ci.yml. The gate enforces a STRICT 3% duplication
threshold on changed Rust source, which catches real copy-paste smells in
services, storage, middleware, etc.
This file is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the duplication-exempt files. The CI
step reads the `ignore` entries under backend/src/api/handlers/ and filters them
out of the changed-file list BEFORE invoking jscpd (deterministic across jscpd
versions). The full `ignore` list also works for ad-hoc local jscpd runs.
To exempt a new format handler, add it to `ignore` below -- nothing else to change.
The per-ecosystem package-format handlers in backend/src/api/handlers/<format>.rs
are DELIBERATELY excluded below. Each one implements the same conceptual shape
(virtual-repo resolve -> download/proxy -> publish/upload) for a different package
ecosystem, so they are inherently structural near-duplicates of one another.
On main with no changes this family already measures ~18% cross-file duplication
(dominant clones are whole-file matches like sbt.rs <-> vscode.rs, including the
shared import block), which is far above the 3% rule. Without this exclusion, ANY
PR that legitimately edits the download/upload path across several formats trips
the gate regardless of code quality, pressuring contributors toward forbidden
--admin merges (see issue #1704).
This is tracked design debt: collapsing the format handlers onto a shared generic
handler is a separate refactor. Until then we suppress the known-structural
duplication HERE only, and keep the gate meaningful everywhere else.
Do NOT add non-format handlers (admin, auth, users, repositories, ...) or anything
under services/, storage/, middleware/ to this ignore list -- duplication there is
a real smell and must keep failing the gate.
